summary: Matched several early Mario fighter functions by moving them from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.s into new C source and header files, then wiring ftmario.o into the source object lists. The PR covered ftMario_OnDeath, func_800E0920, ftMario_OnLoad, an inline-asm wrapper label lbl_800E0A00, and func_800E0A20. It also introduced a partial ftMarioAttributes struct sufficient for the matched code. Review feedback caught a copied header guard typo that initially referenced ftness instead of ftmario; the author fixed it.
what_changed: Removed 163 lines of hand-written/disassembled assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.s for the matched Mario functions at the start of the file, leaving later asm beginning at func_800E0B00.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.c with C implementations for ftMario_OnDeath, func_800E0920, ftMario_OnLoad, and func_800E0A20.
Kept lbl_800E0A00 as an asm function inside ftmario.c rather than converting it to normal C; it simply calls func_800E1368 with the original prologue/epilogue sequence.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.h with includes, prototypes, and a partial ftMarioAttributes struct containing padding through offset 0x14 and field x14.
Updated e_files.mk to add $(EPILOGUE_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED.

Updated obj_files.mk to add $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.o before the remaining asm ftmario.o, allowing this file to be partially decompiled while preserving unmatched asm in the same character module.
smart_moves: Used a partial character-specific attributes struct with explicit padding and the one observed field x14 instead of over-claiming the full Mario attribute layout.
Preserved matching for a troublesome tiny wrapper by keeping lbl_800E0A00 in inline asm, avoiding wasted effort or compiler mismatch risk for a function that only branches to func_800E1368.
Split ftmario into both source and asm object entries, a common partial-decomp tactic when only a prefix of an asm file is matched and later functions remain in assembly.

Expressed the repeated special-attributes copy as typed struct assignment: copying ft->x10C_ftData->ext_attr into ft->x2D8_specialAttributes2 and then assigning ft->x2D4_specialAttributes to that buffer.
Used #pragma push / #pragma peephole on around func_800E0A20, indicating a compiler-codegen tactic needed for the switch/call structure to match the original assembly.
decomp_lessons: For fighter OnLoad functions, watch for character special attributes being copied from ftData->ext_attr into x2D8_specialAttributes2, then stored through x2D4_specialAttributes; this pattern can often be modeled with a character-specific attribute struct even if only a few offsets are known.
Header guards are easy to mis-copy when cloning another fighter header; reviewers expect the guard name to match the file/module, e.g. _ftmario_h_ for ftmario.h rather than _ftness_h_.
Partial decompilation can coexist with an existing asm file by adding a src object while keeping the asm object in obj_files.mk; remove only the matched functions from the asm file.
Item/resource initialization in fighter OnLoad may access ftDataInfo->x48_items and call item/resource helpers such as func_8026B3F8 with constants or character attributes.

Switch reconstruction for item-type behavior may require attention to exact case ordering and compiler behavior; func_800E0A20 maps func_8026B320 results 1..4 to func_80070FB4 third arguments 1, 0, 2, and 3 respectively, then conditionally calls func_80070C48 when arg1 is nonzero.
naming_conventions: Character file/header names follow lowercase fighter module names: ftmario.c and ftmario.h.
Exported lifecycle hooks use the character name with capitalized character portion, as in ftMario_OnDeath and ftMario_OnLoad.
Unknown functions retained address-based names such as func_800E0920, func_800E0A20, func_800E1368, func_8026B3F8, func_8026B2B4, and func_8026B320.
Labels that remained as standalone asm wrappers can retain lbl_ address names, e.g. lbl_800E0A00.
The new character attributes typedef follows the pattern ftMarioAttributes, with unknown fields represented using offset-style names and padding arrays such as x0_padding, x14, and x14_padding.
Header guard convention for this file is _ftmario_h_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed matched assembly from ftmario.s while leaving the remaining unmatched asm in place starting at func_800E0B00.
Used inline asm with nofralloc for lbl_800E0A00 to preserve exact stack frame and branch sequence.
Used #pragma push, #pragma peephole on, and #pragma pop around func_800E0A20, implying peephole optimization was necessary or helpful for matching.
Modeled a 0x84-byte attribute block with padding so struct assignment produced the original copy loop behavior for the special attributes.
Maintained both $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.o and $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.o in TEXT_O_FILES for a partial source/asm split.
review_feedback: PsiLupan reviewed src/melee/ft/chara/ftmario.h and said: "Fix the define here to be ftmario" because the new header guard hunk initially showed _ftness_h_.
nicolasrmerz replied: "Good catch, fixed". The final diff excerpt shows the corrected guard as #ifndef _ftmario_h_ / #define _ftmario_h_.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or flesh out ftMarioAttributes fields beyond x14.
Find other fighter OnLoad functions that copy ext_attr into x2D8_specialAttributes2 using struct assignment for reusable matching patterns.
Search for other PRs using #pragma peephole on around switch statements to understand MWCC matching behavior.
Check whether lbl_800E0A00 was later converted from inline asm to C or given a semantic name.
Review neighboring Mario functions after func_800E0B00 for continued ftmario.s decompilation patterns.
